name: Binary Search on the Rotation
summary: One side of the pivot is always sorted; compare nums[mid] to nums[right] to discard half the range each step — O(log n).
visualizationIntent: init: show the whole rotated array with left at index 0 and right at the last index; the search window covers everything. loop: each step compute mid and probe nums[mid] against nums[right]. check: if nums[mid] > nums[right] the minimum must be to the right of mid, so discard the left half (left = mid + 1); otherwise the minimum is at mid or to its left (right = mid). update/move: the window shrinks by half and left/right glide inward, dimming the discarded side. return: left and right meet on the minimum, which flashes as the result.

lineExplanations.3: Start the left boundary at the first index.
lineExplanations.4: Start the right boundary at the last index.
lineExplanations.5: Keep narrowing while the window still spans more than one element.
lineExplanations.6: Look at the middle index of the current window.
lineExplanations.7: Compare the middle value with the rightmost value to see which half is sorted.
lineExplanations.8: nums[mid] > nums[right]: the minimum is to the right of mid, so move left past mid.
lineExplanations.10: Otherwise the minimum is at mid or to its left, so pull right in to mid.
lineExplanations.11: The window has collapsed onto one index — read the minimum there.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def findMin(self, nums): nums is the rotated sorted array.
syntaxExplanations.3: left = 0 starts the search window at the first index.
syntaxExplanations.4: right = len(nums) - 1 sets the window's right end to the last index.
syntaxExplanations.5: while left < right: continues until the window holds a single element.
syntaxExplanations.6: mid = (left + right) // 2 is the integer midpoint of the window.
syntaxExplanations.7: if nums[mid] > nums[right]: tests whether the right half is unsorted (holds the pivot).
syntaxExplanations.8: left = mid + 1 discards mid and everything left of it.
syntaxExplanations.9: else: the complementary case (bare else — no step of its own).
syntaxExplanations.10: right = mid keeps mid as a candidate and discards everything to its right.
syntaxExplanations.11: answer = nums[left] reads the minimum once left meets right.
syntaxExplanations.12: return answer hands back the minimum.
